target.title: feat(slack): add ignoreOtherMentions config option for channels
target.body:
## Summary

Closes #89625

Adds \`ignoreOtherMentions\` support to Slack channel config, matching the existing Discord behavior.

When \`ignoreOtherMentions: true\` is set on a Slack channel entry, the bot drops incoming room messages that mention another user but do not mention the bot itself (explicitly or implicitly). This prevents accidental bot responses in busy shared channels where users frequently @-mention each other.

**Config example:**
\`\`\`json
{
  "channels": {
    "slack": {
      "channels": {
        "C0ABC12345": {
          "ignoreOtherMentions": true
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
\`\`\`

## Changes

- \`extensions/slack/src/monitor/channel-config.ts\` — add \`ignoreOtherMentions?: boolean\` to \`SlackChannelConfigEntry\` and \`SlackChannelConfigResolved\`; resolve with \`firstDefined\` (supports wildcard fallback)
- \`extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.ts\` — add drop check after the \`allowBots=mentions\` guard, mirroring Discord's implementation
- \`extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.test.ts\` — 4 new unit tests

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Slack bots with \`requireMention: false\` respond to all channel messages including those that mention other users, causing noise and accidental bot responses in busy shared channels. No config existed to suppress this without disabling ambient responses entirely.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw gateway running locally with a Slack dev app connected to a test workspace channel \`#general\` (channel ID \`C07KXYZ1234\`), \`requireMention: false\`, \`ignoreOtherMentions: true\` set on the channel config entry. Node.js v22, OpenClaw from current branch.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Sent the message \`hey <@U04NABCDEF> can you review this?\` in the \`#general\` channel (mentioning another workspace member, not the bot). Bot did not respond.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Redacted gateway verbose log showing the drop:
\`\`\`
[openclaw:slack] verbose: slack: inbound message C07KXYZ1234 ts=1748952301.112400 user=U08RABCDEF text="hey <@U04NABCDEF> can you review this?"
[openclaw:slack] verbose: slack: drop channel=C07KXYZ1234 reason="other-mention (ignoreOtherMentions=true)"
\`\`\`
Sending a message without any user mention in the same channel (\`what is the deploy status?\`) produced a normal bot reply with no drop log line.
- Observed result after fix: Messages mentioning other users (but not the bot) are dropped silently. The gateway log shows \`reason="other-mention (ignoreOtherMentions=true)"\`. Messages with no mentions, or messages that explicitly mention the bot, pass through normally and receive a reply.
- What was not tested: Subteam/usergroup mention interaction (\`<!subteam^...>\`) was not explicitly tested — the implementation only gates on \`<@Uxxx>\` user mentions, matching the Discord behavior which also only checks user/role mentions.

## Tests and validation

Which commands ran:

- \`npx vitest run --project extension-slack src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.test.ts\`
- \`check-test-types\` CI shard (TypeScript)

What regression coverage was added:

4 new unit tests: drop on other-user mention with \`ignoreOtherMentions=true\`, pass-through on bot mention, pass-through on no mentions, opt-out with \`ignoreOtherMentions=false\`.
